title: Select ('S')

Select ('S')

Select the records from a table that match a specified profile.

Description

Uniface uses the stepped hitlist mechanism for selecting records from the database. The Select request can be called multiple times to pass the matching records in a number of steps. The DBMS or the connector must maintain a state to determine which records must be added to the hitlist.

Uniface has two modes of operation: the select cache enabled or disabled.

With the select cache enabled, complete records can be selected and added to the hitlist. With the select cache disabled, only the primary keys or the PRA can be added to it.

Service functions are available to add entries to the hitlist.

Table 1. Modes
Mode Description
Mode 0 Select the first step in the hitlist.

Select records that match the profile specified in the if list and match the expression that may be available in uctrl->umisc, from the table that corresponds to the current uctrl structure. Add every selected record to the Uniface hitlist.

In the following cases, all matching records must be selected:

  • uctrl->umaxhits is 0.
  • uctrl->uonum > 0 (order by is specified) and the DBMS cannot sort records itself.

In all other cases, select at least the number of records as specified in uctrl->umaxhits, or all records if there are less records available. If uctrl->uonum > 0 (order by specified) and the DBMS can sort records itself, set uctrl->uonum to 0 and add the records to the hitlist in the requested order.

If this is successful, return the number of selected records to Uniface.
Mode 1 Identical to mode 0, but do not add records to the hitlist.
Mode 2 If at least one record matches the profile, return 1 to Uniface. Do not add records to the hitlist.
Mode 3 Select the next step in the hitlist, considering the selection started with the last Select mode 0. Add every selected record to the Uniface hitlist. If uctrl->umaxhits is 0, all remaining records must be selected. Otherwise, select at least the number of records as specified in uctrl->umaxhits, or all remaining records if fewer records are available.

If the connector indicated that the DBMS handles sorting by setting uctrl->uonum to 0 in the mode 0 request, add the records to the hitlist in the requested order. Set uctrl->uonum to 0 to indicate that the step is ordered by the DBMS.

If no error occurred, return the number of selected records to Uniface.

Note: If umaxhits was set to 0 in the Info request, the connector is never requested to perform Select mode 3.
Mode 4 Compute an aggregate function.

For this mode, the selection is specified in the if list and the computation is specified in the uordlst structure which has uctrl->uonum elements. For each element in uordlst, uorder specifies the computation to be performed and ufnr specifies the field to which the computation applies.

The action to be taken depends on the capabilities of the DBMS.

If the DBMS is capable of making the selection and computing the aggregate functions, call the usumfld service function for every computation specified in the uordlst.

If the DBMS is capable of making the selection but not of computing the aggregate function, select the fields specified in uordlst and call the usumhit service function.

If the DBMS is not capable of making the selection, select all records in the table and call the uhitchk service function.

If the action is successful, return the number of selected records to Uniface.

From Uniface

Preconditions realized by Uniface—the table, for which the Select is requested, is open.

Table 2. Information available from Uniface
Data Structure->Field Description Mode
0 1 2 3 4
uctrl->udrv Points to the connector workspace if a workspace was requested in the Info call. X X X X X
uctrl->udrvini The unique identification of the current logon path. X X X X X
uctrl->uiflst Points to the if list. X X X X X
uctrl->uifnum The number of elements in the if list. If uifnum is 0, all records in the table must be selected, although uctrl->umisc may still impose a restriction as described below. X X X X X
uctrl->uflist The field list which describes the entity and all the fields. X X X X X
uctrl->ufnum The number of entries in the field list. X X X X X
uctrl->iobuf Points to the general I/O buffer. X X X X X
uctrl->sqlbuf Points to the SQL buffer. X X X X X
uctrl->TMEnabled Indicates whether the connector is TM-controlled. X X X X X
uctrl->uwlist Points to the where structure. X X X X X
uctrl->umaxhits Number of records to select in this step. If 0, select all matching records. X X X
uctrl->umisc May point to a null-terminated string which is an expression in the query language of the DBMS. Only records which match this expression must be selected. If the DBMS connector does not support a query language, the connector may ignore this field. X X X X
uctrl->uolist Points to the order by list. X X
uctrl->uonum Number of entries in the order by list. X X
uctrl->ucache If 0, the select cache is disabled. Otherwise it is enabled.

If this field is nonzero, the Uniface select cache can be used by means of the uaddrec service function. Otherwise, it is not useful to select complete records, although it is still allowed to call uaddrec.

 X X
uctrl->uonum Number of elements in the uordlst structure. X
uctrl->uolist Selection and computation to be performed. X
uordlst->ufnr Field number of the field to which the computation applies. X
uordlst->uorder Computation to be performed. For details, see Select request uordlst->uorder tables. X

uordlst->uorder

Table 3. Computations to be performed
Uorder Computation Description
0 Transport Pass the value of the specified field of the last selected record to Uniface.
1 Sum Compute the sum of the specified field in the selected records.
2 Max Compute the maximum value of the specified field in the selected records.
3 Min Compute the minimum value of the specified field in the selected records.
4 Count Compute the number of selected records that contain a value in the specified field.
5 Average Compute the average value of the specified field in the selected records.

The following table shows which functions can be used with which Uniface data types.

Table 4. Selected db functions and data types
Data type max min sum ave count
String No No No No Yes
Raw No No No No Yes
Numeric Yes Yes Yes Yes Yes
Float Yes Yes Yes Yes Yes
Date Yes Yes No No Yes
Time Yes Yes No No Yes
Datetime Yes Yes No No Yes
Boolean Yes Yes No No Yes

To Uniface

In the Select call mode 0 and 3 with the select cache disabled, the connector only needs to retrieve the following data from the DBMS.

  • If uctrl->urdbykey is 0: the PRA
  • If uctrl->urdbykey is not zero: the primary key fields
  • The fields involved in the specified selection profile if the DBMS is not capable of making the requested selection
  • The fields involved in the order by clause if the DBMS cannot sort records itself

In the Select call mode 0 and 3 with the select cache enabled, the connector needs to retrieve the following data from the DBMS:

  • If uctrl->urdbykey is nonzero: the complete record
  • If uctrl->urdbykey is 0: the complete record and the PRA

When fields are selected, they must be stored in the general I/O buffer at the offset as available in ufldlst->ufpos. When a PRA is selected, it must be stored in a local buffer (there is no space reserved for the PRA in the I/O buffer), and passed to Uniface using the uaddhit or uaddrec functions.

Table 5. Connector Request Return Values
Value Description
>=0 Number of selected records.
-3 Exceptional error.
-9 I/O server logon error.
-16 DBMS reported network error.

If the connector request return value is less than 0, an error code must be supplied in uctrl->udrverr.

Service Functions

  • uaddhit — Add the primary key or the PRA of a record, or both, to the hitlist.
  • uaddrec — Add a complete record to the Uniface select cache.
  • uhitchk — Check if a record matches the specified selection profile. Implicitly performs uaddhit and usumhit in the appropriate modes.
  • uready — Use with an ISAM-like DBMS in combination with uhitchk to stop searching through the preferred index.
  • usumhit — Compute the requested aggregate function for the records it is called with.
  • usumfld — Provide Uniface with the result value of an aggregate computation.

Extra information

Because of optimizing performed within Uniface, the value of uctrl->umaxhits available in the Select request can be different from the value supplied with the Info request.

The implementation of the Select request must ensure that a Select mode 0 for all records results in exactly the same hitlist as a complete sequence of stepped Selects.

When a Select mode 0 is requested, any pending Select sequence on the same table has either been completed by Uniface, or can be safely discarded.

The optional expression in uctrl->umisc comes from the ProcScript read where statement. The connector is responsible for integrating the expression with the expression in the if list.

Uniface uses Select mode 1 to count the number of matching records (for example, when the ProcScript command lookup is executed).

Select mode 2 is an existence check which is used to implement referential integrity. An efficient implementation of mode 2 returns to Uniface as soon as a record is found.

A sequence of Select mode 0 and mode 3 calls can be mixed with calls to all other connector requests, except Rollback, Commit, Close, Open, Logon, Logoff and other Select modes. The state that is maintained in the Select request should therefore not be changed by the other connector requests.

Select mode 4 is used when the ProcScript command selectdb is executed.

If the DBMS is not capable of making the requested selection, the connector can use the service function uhitchk to check if a record matches the specified profile. It is recommended to let the DBMS make the selection whenever possible.

Uniface can change the value of uctrl->ucache in a sequence of Select mode 0 and mode 3 requests. An efficient implementation of the Select request uses the select cache when possible, but does not retrieve complete records when the select cache is not used.

When the connector adds fewer hits than requested in uctrl->umaxhits in mode 0 and 3, Uniface assumes that the last record has been selected.

TM-Controlled Connectors

TM-controlled connectors are not allowed to begin, commit or rollback transactions.

Also, connectors which are TM-controlled do not receive Commit/Rollback requests. Therefore, connectors which normally perform cursor administration at the end of a transaction may not be able to determine the state of a cursor when the connector is TM-controlled.

The following is recommended for TM-controlled connectors:

  • In select mode 0, do not reuse a cursor from a previous hitlist. Instead, close the cursor and open it again. It is possible that an error may occur if the cursor was implicitly closed by the DBMS at the end of a transaction. In this case, the connector can choose to ignore DBMS errors which indicate that the cursor is already closed.
  • Always close the cursor after all the data for a hitlist has been retrieved.
Related Links
  • uctrl

    Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table.

  • uordlst

    Describes the requested ordering of selected records.

  • TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors

    A database connector can be controlled by a transaction manager only if it is TM-capable. Uniface does not provide TM-controlled connectors and does not directly support third-party transaction managers. However, you can use the APIs provided by the Database Connector Interface to build a custom database connector that does.

Was this topic helpful?
LikeDislike
